refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates communication quality management within the protocol layer, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints to FR or DP to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware modules responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and control inputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical actuation, low containment of communication logic.
High containment of teleoperation communication functions, low diffusion risk.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, minimal communication protocol containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link modules to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to strong containment within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support the assignment; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network packet analysis within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for communication quality to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add latency performance constraints to FR and DP to address teleoperation responsiveness.
Define interface protocols explicitly between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety alerts.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding latency constraints and clarifying safety interface protocols will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and safety interface details for improved completeness and risk mitigation.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement adaptive protocols effectively.
open_risks: Integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under adaptive protocol operation.
Interface validation between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
